MESONET STATION CRWC2 THE CROWN, BASALT 3WSW.
COVERED THE GROUND UP TO 6 INCHES.
MESA VERDE NP PERSONNEL REPORTED HAIL LASTED UNTIL APPROX. 220 PM MDT. HAIL DIMPLED AT LEAST 2 VEHICLES.
1.5 INCHES OF HAIL ON GROUND.
PUBLIC REPORT WITH PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. SIZE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 1.5 AND 2 INCHES.
ASOS STATION KRIL GARFIELD COUNTY RGNL AP.
MUDSLIDE OVER CO HIGHWAY 145.
3 FEET OF RUNNING WATER ON COUNTY ROAD 24 AND 24A FROM HEAVY RAIN AND RUN-OFF. THIS CORRECTS THE LOCATION OF PREVIOUS REPORT.
COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED 3 FEET OF RUNNING WATER OVER COUNTY ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED.
COUNTY ROAD 4 WASHED OUT DUE TO HEAVY RAIN AND RUN-OFF.
ASOS STATION KCNY 16 NW MOAB.

ADDITIONAL DETAILS FROM PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM SAWPIT. MUDSLIDE REPORTED BETWEEN MILEMARKERS 77 AND 80 NEAR THE SAWPIT AREA ON HWY 145. ADDITIONALLY, HAD FALLING ROCKS AND DEBRIS. ROCKS WERE THE SIZE OF CARS AND WATER WAS 2 TO 3 FEET DEEP IN SOME SPOTS.
MUDSLIDE REPORTED NEAR MILEMARKER 80 NEAR THE SAWPIT AREA ON HWY 145.
STREET FLOODING HAS OCCURRED, DRAINS NOT ABLE TO KEEP UP WITH THE HEAVY RAINS.
WATER FLOWING ON STREETS DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.
WATER FLOWING IN NORMALLY DRY RAVINES NEAR EAST END OF THE COLORADO NATIONAL MONUMENT.
WATER ON STREETS IN TOWN DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.

DEBRIS FLOW IN PACK CREEK CAUSED FLOODING OVER SEVERAL ROADWAYS ALONG THE CREEK. THERE WAS ALSO HIGH WATER AND POSSIBLE DEBRIS IN MILL CREEK AS WELL. HIGH WATER MOVED THROUGH SPANISH VALLEY AND MOAB. NO STRUCTURES IMPACTED.

A MUDSLIDE OCCURRED ALONG HIGHWAY 550 ON RED MOUNTAIN PASS NEAR IRONTON AS A RESULT OF HEAVY RAIN. DOT REPORTED APPROXIMATELY 3 FEET OF MUD AND DEBRIS INCLUDING DEAD TREES, LIMBS AND BOULDERS ON THE ROAD.
ASOS STATION KEEO MEEKER AIRPORT.
DISPATCH CALLED TO REPORT FLASH FLOODING FROM THE PACK CREEK BURN SCAR. SUNNY ACRES, AS WELL AS OLD CITY PARK RD WERE CLOSED.
MUDSLIDE OCCURRED AT MM 57 ON HWY 133 NEAR REDSTONE, CO.
DEBRIS FLOW ON COUNTY ROAD 501.
MUDSLIDE ON HIGHWAY 133. BOTH LANES OF TRAFFIC CLOSED.
MUTIPLE DEBRIS FLOWS ON FS RD 314. THE DEBRIS WAS 18 FEET DEEP IN SPOTS.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED FLASH FLOODING AT MILE MARKER 130 ON HIGHWAY 160, TRIGGERING A MUDSLIDE WITH 6 TO 8 INCHES OF WATER AND MUD RUNNING ACROSS THE HIGHWAY.

WATER RAN ACROSS FOREST SERVICE ROAD 520 NEAR HIGHWAY 149 NEAR THE HINSDALE AND MINERAL COUNTY LINE DUE TO HEAVY RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORMS. 5 FEET OF STANDING WATER ON THE ROAD RESULTED FROM A BLOCKED CULVERT, CAUSING A VEHICLE TO BE TRAPPED.
A MUDSLIDE OCCURRED NORTH OF THE VALLECITO MARINA & YACHT CLUB AS A RESULT OF HEAVY RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORMS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
SEVERAL SIDE STREETS AND LOW LYING AREAS FLOODED ALONG MILL AND PACK CREEKS. WATER RAN OUT OF BANKS IN MANY SPOTS. MILL CREEK STREAM GAUGE IN MOAB INCREASED BY 9 FEET IN 2 HOURS.
DEBRIS FLOW STARTED ON THE PACK CREEK BURN SCAR AS MEASURED BY A USGS STREAM GAUGE.
MUD AND ROCKS FLOWED ACROSS DESERT SOLITAIRE ROAD WHERE IT CROSSES PACK CREEK AS A RESULT OF HEAVY RAIN OVER THE PACK CREEK FIRE BURN SCAR.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED TO BE BETWEEN 55-60 MPH CAUSED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE IN THE NAVAJO RIDGE NEIGHBORHOOD OUTSIDE MOAB. SOLAR PANELS WERE DAMAGED.
FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED AT THE INTERSECTION OF PACK CREEK ROAD AND LA SAL LOOP ROAD AS A RESULT OF HEAVY RAIN OVER THE PACK CREEK FIRE BURN SCAR. WATER PUMMELED THE BRIDGE AND FLOWED OVER THE ROAD.
VIDEO ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF FLASH FLOODING ON KANE SPRINGS RD. TIME ESTIMATED.
VIDEO ON SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED.
HWY PATROL REPORTED WATER OVER ROADWAY ON US-191 AND UT-211 WITH VEHICLES STRANDED IN THE AREA.
PART OF COUNTY ROAD 211 WASHED OUT. ROAD WILL BE CLOSED OVERNIGHT.
VERY MINOR MUD SLIDE BARELY IMPACTING HWY 145.
CDOT REPORTED A MUDSLIDE ON HIGHWAY 145.
MUDSLIDE OVER COUNTY ROAD 3 REPORT FROM LOCAL SHERIFF.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 8 NW CANYONLANDS NP. WHITE RIM ROAD WAS WASHED OUT AND THERE IS A VEHICLE HANGING OVER THE EDGE.
DEBRIS FLOW ON SHAFER BASIN ROAD. THERE IS STILL A LOT OF DEBRIS ON THE ROAD MAKING FOR DANGEROUS TRAVEL.
VIDEO SHARED ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF SEVERAL FEET OF WATER FLOWING THROUGH A NEIGHBORHOOD.
WHITE RIM ROAD WAS WASHED OUT AND THERE IS A VEHICLE HANGING OVER THE EDGE.
1.75 INCHES OF RAIN IN LESS THAN AN HOUR. PACK CREEK AND MILL CREEK OVERFLOWED THEIR BANKS AND FLOODED DOWNTOWN. ROAD GRADERS OUT ALL NIGHT AND STILL ARE.
DEBRIS AND ACCUMULATED HAIL CLEARED OFF INTERSTATE 70 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 168 AND 170 FROM EAST BOUND AND WEST BOUND LANES.
ROAD CLOSURES IN SPANISH VALLEY DUE TO FLASH FLOODING OVER ROADWAYS. ROADS OFFICIALLY DECLARED CLOSED OVERNIGHT.
ASOS STATION KCEZ CORTEZ MUNICIPAL AIRPORT.
